rules: RenderMan product names, renderer names, node names, API identifiers, file paths, environment variables, and UI labels with exact spelling must be preserved unless the glossary gives a Chinese rendering.
First occurrence of an important English technical acronym may use Chinese explanation plus acronym, e.g. '任意输出变量（AOV）'; subsequent occurrences may use the acronym.
Do not translate identifiers beginning with Pxr, Rix, Ri:, RfM, RfH, RfK, RfB, PRMan, RMAN, RMANTREE, or node/operator names from DCC applications.
Translate explanatory prose naturally, but keep command-line examples, code blocks, attribute names, parameter names, JSON keys, and file extensions unchanged.
Prefer established CG/VFX Chinese terminology: 渲染, 着色, 材质, 纹理, 采样, 积分器, 光路, 合成, 置换, 体积, 次表面散射, 法线, 反照率, 噪声, 降噪.

protected_terms.note: Common CG/VFX shorthand; can explain as 外观开发.
Hybrid CPU/GPU RenderMan renderer; explain as CPU/GPU 混合渲染器 when needed.
RenderMan Integrator System; usually keep acronym.
Digital Content Creation; may explain as 数字内容创作软件.
Universal Scene Description; first occurrence may be 通用场景描述（USD）.
Open Shading Language; first occurrence may be 开放着色语言（OSL）.
Arbitrary Output Variable; first occurrence may be 任意输出变量（AOV）.
Light Path Expression; first occurrence may be 光路表达式（LPE）.

items.text: The single GUI program integrated RenderMan Installer is not the appropriate way to install RenderMan on medium to large numbers of machines. The RenderMan Installer downloads and installs individual component installers, but these individual component installers are available separately from the
forum website
(forum login required).
Obtaining a License File
There are two ways to obtain a license file if you are not using the RenderMan Installer:
Run the LicenseApp tool and click the "Get License" button. It will prompt for your forum user name, password and serial number and download the license file.
Go to the
forum webpage
and entering your forum user name, password, serial number, hostname and hostid. The hostid can be determined either by using the LicenseApp tool or by running the license server with a special flag:
If you are using Node-Locked licenses you will need a separate license file for each machine. If you are using a floating license you only need one file which maybe be copied as needed.
License File Location

The license file's name is pixar.license and ordinarily goes next the the distribution directories. For example, if you use the default install locations on each operating system the license file would be:
Linux
Windows
macOS
Floating License Considerations
Refer to
Installing The License Server
for more detailed information on how to setup the floating license services and starting and stopping and configuring them.

In a floating license installation one machine of the network needs to have the PixarLicenseServer installed and running. Each workstation that is to use the license must have continuous network connectivity to this machine. There are several ways that each workstation can be directed to the license server machine. Choose which method best on which fits into your environment:

Each workstation can simply have a copy of the pixar.license file in the default location next to distributions as described above. The license file contains enough information for the software (prman or RenderMan for Maya, etc) to determine it is a network license and what the address of license server is. If you do not wish to install the pixar.license file in the default location you may set the environment variable PIXAR_LICENSE_FILE to the full path name of the license file.
The workstation can be set so the environment variable PIXAR_LICENSE_FILE containing the port number and hostname value which avoids the need for any file on the client workstation.
The value of /licenseserver in your rendermn.ini file can be set to the location
The address of your license server might need to be of the form hostname.company.com or even the address like 10.0.0.2, or even localhost, if you do not have a DNS server entry for it.

The LicenseApp tool is aware of all of these methods of finding the license server so it is a good way to test and monitor the status of a license from any workstation. It is designed to be helpful to diagnose situations such as you have PIXAR_LICENSE_FILE set but to a file that is unreadable or the license server is not reachable via the network.
